one_time_description: Impairment loss on investments: $999,975 on Settlement Trust and $2,500,000 on Business Enterprises
economic_notes: First Nation is economically dependent on its Business Enterprises (Fort McKay Group of Companies LP and Fort McKay Landing LP) primarily in oil sands service and development. Business enterprises had aggregate revenue of $445,463,498 and net income of $54,760,641 in 2024. Subsequent to year-end, the First Nation withdrew $18,000,000 from Settlement Trust to distribute to members, and entered into agreement to sell investment in Hammerstone Infrastructure Materials Ltd. for approximately $3,100,000.
notes: Fort McKay First Nation has two GBEs accounted for under modified equity method: Fort McKay Group of Companies LP and Fort McKay Landing LP, both held through the Fort McKay Investment Trust. Business enterprise aggregate revenue was $445M. Impairment losses totaling $3,499,975 recorded on investments (Settlement Trust $999,975 and Business Enterprises $2,500,000). Subsequent event: $18M withdrawn from Settlement Trust post year-end for member distributions. No schedule of remuneration included in the financial statements. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC ($13,223,282) + Health Canada ($2,120,359) + CMHC ($205,762) + Athabasca Tribal Council ($742,819) + First Nations Development Funding ($2,289,544) = $18,581,766; however ATC may not be government transfer so conservative estimate used. osr calculated as total revenue minus total_government_transfers.
